title: America’s next economic frontier is 240,000 miles away
summary: The article discusses the economic potential of the Moon as a frontier for America, highlighting its significance 240,000 miles away. It reflects on past achievements like the Apollo missions and the renewed interest in lunar exploration.

title: China conducts 4 launches in 3 days, but silence follows Kuaizhou–11 launch
summary: China conducted four launches in three days, but a long silence followed the Kuaizhou-11 solid rocket launch, indicating potential issues. The accelerated launch pace highlights China's growing space activity.

title: Dawn Aerospace raises $25 million
summary: Dawn Aerospace secured $25 million in funding to expand its operations in in-space transportation and suborbital spaceplanes. The investment will support scaling up the company's innovative spaceplane technology.

title: Look Up and Skynopy partner on automated satellite collision avoidance service
summary: Look Up and Skynopy have partnered to automate a satellite collision avoidance service using Skynopy's ground station network. The collaboration aims to enhance safety in low Earth orbit.

title: June 25:  Golden Dome: How Could Sensors Protect the United States?
summary: The article discusses the Golden Dome satellite program and the sensor technologies used to protect the United States. It explores the operational capabilities and future possibilities of these satellites.

title: Astrobotic unveils Griffin-1 lunar lander
summary: Astrobotic has revealed its Griffin-1 lunar lander, which is set to launch later this year as part of NASA's lunar base initiatives. The lander will serve as a key component in advancing the agency's plans for sustained lunar exploration.

title: Swiss decision to not contribute to Copernicus tests program’s value model
summary: Switzerland has decided not to participate in the EU's Copernicus Earth observation program for the 2028–2034 funding cycle, sparking discussions about the program's value. The move highlights ongoing debates over international contributions to European space initiatives.

title: The SpaceNews space unicorn tracker
summary: SpaceNews has released an ongoing list of private space companies valued at $1 billion or more, often referred to as 'space unicorns'.

title: Katalyst Space raises $12 million for GEO servicing demo mission
summary: Katalyst Space Technologies raised $12 million for a geosynchronous orbit demonstration mission aimed at boosting the orbit of a NASA observatory. The funding supports their upcoming launch and satellite servicing efforts.

title: PiLogic partners with Air Force lab to test satellite fault-prediction software
summary: PiLogic has partnered with an Air Force lab to test its satellite fault-prediction software, which uses probabilistic reasoning to diagnose spacecraft anomalies.

title: A volcanic eruption seen from space | Space photo of the day for June 17, 2026
summary: A volcanic eruption on Hawaii's Kīlauea volcano was captured in a space photo on June 17, 2026. This marks the 49th known eruption of the volcano.

title: 'Best. Mars. Mission. Ever.' Scientists hail MAVEN's legacy as NASA retires Red Planet orbiter
summary: NASA's MAVEN Mars mission concluded after 11 years, having uncovered insights into how Mars lost its atmosphere and served as a critical communications relay for surface missions.

title: Stream Season 3 of Silo in safety, anywhere, with 70% off 24 months of ProtonVPN Plus
summary: ProtonVPN is offering a 70% discount on 24 months of ProtonVPN Plus to promote safe streaming of 'Silo' Season 3. The deal encourages secure online viewing of the post-apocalyptic series.

title: NASA discussing bold mission to boost Swift space telescope today: Listen live
summary: NASA is discussing an ambitious mission to boost the orbit of its Swift space telescope during a press conference. The event is scheduled for June 17 and will be available to listen live.

title: NASA X-ray spacecraft catches jet erupting from 1st supermassive black hole imaged by humanity
summary: NASA's X-ray spacecraft has captured a jet erupting from the first supermassive black hole imaged by humanity. The observation provides unprecedented detail in X-rays, revealing changes in the jet.

title: European rocket launches record-breaking mission with Amazon LEO satellites
summary: An Ariane 6 heavy-lift rocket launched a record-breaking payload to orbit, carrying a batch of Amazon's LEO satellites. This mission marks a significant milestone for European space capabilities and Amazon's satellite internet ambitions.

title: Look up! The moon and Venus will stage a rare daytime disappearing act today
summary: The moon will briefly obscure Venus in a rare daytime occultation on June 17, visible from parts of North America. This celestial event is a unique alignment of the moon and Venus.

title: The Nikon Z fc is our choice as the best budget camera — this retro-inspired beauty is at its best price of the year in this early Amazon Prime Day deal
summary: Nikon Z fc is highlighted as the best budget camera in an Amazon Prime Day deal. The retro-inspired mirrorless APS-C camera is praised for its looks and performance.

title: Watch a fireball burn a 300 mile path above the Midwest US (video)
summary: A fireball streaked across the Midwest US, traveling 300 miles in seconds before disintegrating. The event was captured on video, showcasing a spectacular display.

title: Hubble Sees Swarm of Galaxies
summary: The NASA Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the galaxy cluster MACS0329-0211, resembling a swarm of bees. Galaxy clusters like this are crucial for understanding the evolution of the universe's structure.

title: NASA’s Webb Catches Exoplanet Getting Roasted
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope observed an exoplanet, HD 80606 b, experiencing extreme heating due to its elliptical orbit. Researchers are analyzing the data to understand the planet's atmospheric conditions.

title: Astronaut Jessica Meir Assists With Hardware Updates for NASA’s Cold Atom Lab
summary: NASA astronaut Jessica Meir installed hardware updates for the Cold Atom Lab (CAL) aboard the International Space Station. CAL cools atoms to near absolute zero to study quantum phenomena.

title: NASA Webb, Hubble Reveal History of Relic of Milky Way’s Formation
summary: Researchers used NASA's James Webb and Hubble Space Telescopes to reveal that Terzan 5 is not a globular star cluster, providing new insights into galaxy formation. This discovery challenges previous classifications and enhances understanding of the Milky Way's evolution.

title: NASA’s Quantum Lab Aboard Space Station Gets Chilly Upgrade
summary: NASA's Cold Atom Lab aboard the International Space Station has been upgraded to enhance quantum experiments in microgravity. The facility enables unique scientific research on fundamental matter properties and quantum technologies.

title: NASA Uses Machine Learning to Enhance Flash Flood Warnings
summary: NASA has developed the Transient Artifact and Continuous Learning System (TACLS) to improve flash flood warnings by combining satellite data with machine learning models. This system assists meteorologists at the National Weather Service in forecasting flash floods more efficiently.

title: Department of Health and Human Services Digital Stockpile & Manufacturing Response Network Challenge
summary: NASA's Center of Excellence for Collaborative Innovation (CoECI) is assisting the Department of Health and Human Services in a crowdsourcing challenge for stockpile and manufacturing response. The NASA Tournament Lab will manage the challenge on behalf of NASA and other federal agencies.

title: Metrics
summary: NASA has released the FY26 Services Catalog and Service Level Agreement (SLA) detailing service descriptions, chargeback rates, and service level indicators for NSSC services. The documents outline roles, responsibilities, and rates for all NASA services.

title: Aurora Australis
summary: The International Space Station captured a photograph of the aurora australis over Earth during a solar event on June 5, 2026. The image was taken from an altitude of 271 miles above the Indian Ocean southwest of Perth, Australia.

title: Launch Preview: 10 launches scheduled from sites across the world
summary: Ten launches are scheduled worldwide in the upcoming week, marking a busy period for space industry activities.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket after weeks of delays
summary: Isar Aerospace canceled the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket after multiple delays. The European private space launch provider is based in Ottobrunn, Germany.

title: Booster 20 Completes Cryo, As Ship 40 Nears Static Fire
summary: Booster 20 has completed cryogenic testing, while Ship 40 is nearing a static fire test. The focus remains on the progress following Flight 12.

title: China successfully debuts tallest rocket, LandSpace prepares for second landing attempt
summary: China successfully launched its tallest rocket, marking a significant milestone in its space program. LandSpace is preparing for a second attempt at a rocket landing.

title: Japan’s H3 to returns to flight with debut launch of lightest configuration
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight with its debut launch of the lightest configuration. The mission marks a significant milestone for the country's space program after previous setbacks.

title: Stoke Space completes Nova Stage 1 structural verification
summary: Stoke Space has completed the proto-qualification structural verification of the first stage of its Nova rocket. This marks a significant milestone in the development of the company's reusable launch vehicle.

title: Artemis III Crew Named: Veteran Astronauts to test Lunar Landers in LEO
summary: NASA has named the four astronauts who will fly the Artemis III mission to test lunar landers in low Earth orbit (LEO). This crewed mission is a key step toward returning humans to the Moon.

title: Booster 20 into Cryogenic Testing ahead of Starship Flight 13
summary: SpaceX's Starbase teams are advancing preparations for Starship Flight 13, with Booster 20 undergoing cryogenic testing. This follows the recent conclusion of Starship Flight 12.

title: Launch Preview: Chinese, Rocket Lab, and Starlink flights scheduled
summary: China, Rocket Lab, and SpaceX are scheduled to conduct multiple launches in the upcoming week, including three Starlink missions. The flights will originate from China, California, Virginia, and Florida.

title: NASA concludes MAVEN mission at Mars, uses data to discover new atmospheric phenomenon
summary: NASA has concluded the MAVEN mission at Mars after a six-month effort to restore communication. The mission's data has led to the discovery of a new atmospheric phenomenon.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry news item, though the specific details were not provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements. The publication marks five decades of European space industry milestones.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new advanced robotic arm designed for space applications, potentially revolutionizing satellite servicing and maintenance. It highlights the innovation's potential to enhance operational efficiency in orbit.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway, supporting scientific research in space. The programme facilitates participation of research institutes in European space science activities.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance for the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section tests materials for spacecraft to ensure they survive space conditions. The section is supported by young researchers contributing innovative ideas.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical parachute testing process for a space mission, likely involving high-temperature conditions to simulate re-entry stresses. This bake-out process ensures the parachute's reliability and performance during a spacecraft's descent.

title: Instinct Space Unveils Plans for Low-Cost Lunar Landers
summary: Instinct Space has shifted its focus from navigation for lunar missions to developing low-cost lunar landers. The company aims to directly support surface missions.

title: Katalyst Raises $12M to Extend Satellite Servicing to GEO
summary: Katalyst Space Technologies has raised $12 million to expand its satellite servicing capabilities to geostationary orbit (GEO). The company plans to launch a mission closer to Earth this month, followed by its first GEO mission in 2027.

title: Dawn Aerospace Raises $25M Series B
summary: Dawn Aerospace has raised $25 million in a Series B funding round to accelerate the development of its Aurora spaceplane and conduct an in-space refueling demonstration.

title: Bill Spotlight: NEW HORIZON Act
summary: A bipartisan bill called the NEW HORIZON Act was introduced to allow national security missions to utilize commercial orbital data centers. The act aims to leverage the growing trend of commercial space data infrastructure.

title: Optera Raises £3M to Open New UK HQ
summary: Optera secured £3 million in funding to establish a new UK headquarters, aligning with the UK government's push to enhance domestic technical capabilities.

title: PiLogic Partners with AFRL on AI Anomaly Detection Tech
summary: PiLogic has partnered with the Air Force Research Laboratory (AFRL) to develop AI-based anomaly detection technology, currently being tested on terrestrial flat sats with potential expansion into orbit.

title: Space Stocks Dip After SpaceX IPO
summary: SpaceX's successful IPO led to a pullback in other publicly traded space companies, causing major players to decline. Investors shifted focus to SpaceX, impacting broader space sector stocks.

title: JAXA Nails H3 Return to Flight
summary: JAXA successfully launched the H3 rocket on its eighth mission, marking its first successful flight with a new three-engine variant. The rocket delivered all six of its payloads to space from Japan’s Tanegashima Space Center.

title: VCs Predict the SpaceX IPO Will Lift the Entire Industry
summary: Venture capitalists anticipate that SpaceX's potential IPO could significantly boost the entire space industry by attracting more investment. The move may shift reliance from private capital to public markets, benefiting other space companies.

title: Op-ed: Up to a Trillion Dollars is About to Chase Space. What Kind of Economy Will Be Built?
summary: A trillion dollars is expected to flow into the space industry, raising questions about how this capital will shape the future space economy. The focus is on whether investments will drive operational growth or merely accumulate hardware.

title: SpaceX Launches 3 AST SpaceMobile BlueBirds in Stacked Mission
summary: SpaceX successfully launched three AST SpaceMobile BlueBird satellites in a stacked mission, marking the first time three satellites were deployed in a single launch fairing. The mission included BlueBird 8, 9, and 10 satellites.

title: Eutelsat Secures First Order Under French Defense Agreement
summary: Eutelsat has secured its first order under a new agreement with the French Ministry of the Armed Forces and Veterans. The company also announced additional deals, including one for Low-Earth Orbit services.

title: All.Space Relocates to US Amid York Space Acquisition Process
summary: All.Space has relocated from the U.K. to Alabama, aligning with U.S. defense priorities during its acquisition by York Space Systems.

title: Arianespace Launches Record Mission for Amazon Leo
summary: Arianespace conducted a record-breaking mission for Amazon's Leo constellation, deploying the heaviest payload ever carried by an Ariane rocket and the most Amazon Leo satellites in a single launch.

title: Anuvu Names Former HBO Exec as CEO
summary: Anuvu appointed former HBO executive Simon Sutton as its new CEO on June 15, replacing longtime leader Josh Marks. The leadership change follows the departure of former CEO Marks.

title: Gilat to Acquire Comtech’s Satellite and Space Business, Targeting US Defense Growth
summary: Gilat Satellite Networks is acquiring Comtech's Satellite and Space Communications segment to strengthen its position in the US defense market. The deal combines two major players in satellite ground technology.

title: Israel’s El Al Airline to Adopt Starlink for IFC
summary: Israel's El Al Airline will adopt SpaceX's Starlink for in-flight connectivity, with service to be rolled out gradually. The agreement marks a significant step in enhancing passenger connectivity onboard the national airline.

title: SpaceX Begins Trading at $150 Making Elon Musk the World’s First Trillionaire on Paper
summary: SpaceX began public trading on Nasdaq at $150 per share, an 11% increase over its IPO price, making Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire on paper.

title: Satellite IoT is Becoming Essential Infrastructure as NTN and Cellular IoT Services Converge
summary: Satellite IoT is increasingly being recognized as essential infrastructure as Non-Terrestrial Networks (NTN) and cellular IoT services converge, meeting the real-time needs of enterprises managing remote assets. This integration is becoming critical for industries like utilities, industrial sites, and fleet management.

title: U.S. State Department Taps SpaceX’s Starlink for Two-Year Disaster Response Initiative
summary: The U.S. Department of State signed a two-year agreement with SpaceX to use Starlink for global disaster response and humanitarian aid. The initiative aims to improve communication and coordination in crisis situations.
